question_text: Define the term 'colour filter' and explain what it means when a filter is described as 'absorbing' light.
correct_answer: A colour filter is a transparent material that selectively transmits certain colours of light whilst absorbing others. When a filter absorbs light, it means that particular wavelengths (colours) are removed from the beam and do not pass through the filter.
explanation: Colour filters work by letting some colours of light pass through (transmission) while stopping others (absorption). When light is absorbed by a filter, that energy is taken out of the light beam - those colours simply don't come out the other side. For example, a red filter absorbs all colours except red, so only red light passes through.
